title: APT37 (Famous Chollima) - Operation HanKook Phantom Defense
description: APT37 (Famous Chollima) is a North Korean state-sponsored threat actor conducting sophisticated cyber espionage campaigns since 2012. In 2025, they launched Operation HanKook Phantom, targeting South Korean academics and think tanks with spear-phishing attacks deploying RokRat malware. The group has evolved to use AI-driven social engineering, fraudulent recruitment campaigns, and multi-stage attacks for both espionage and financial gain.

simulation_steps.title: Spear-phishing with malicious decoy documents
simulation_steps.description: APT37 sends targeted spear-phishing emails containing malicious decoy documents (HWP, PDF, LNK files) such as 'National Intelligence Research Society Newsletter' to South Korean academics, think tanks, and energy/security associations

simulation_steps.title: Initial payload execution via LNK files
simulation_steps.description: Malicious LNK files are executed on victim systems, initiating the infection chain and preparing for malware deployment

simulation_steps.title: PowerShell-based infection chain deployment
simulation_steps.description: The attack leverages PowerShell scripts to execute the infection chain, downloading and installing the RokRat backdoor on compromised systems

simulation_steps.title: RokRat backdoor installation and persistence
simulation_steps.description: RokRat malware is installed with persistence mechanisms including scheduled tasks, registry modifications, and remote management tools to maintain long-term access

simulation_steps.title: Cloud-based C2 communication establishment
simulation_steps.description: The malware establishes encrypted command and control communications using compromised cloud services including Dropbox, Yandex, and pCloud for data exfiltration and remote control

simulation_steps.title: Data collection and exfiltration
simulation_steps.description: Sensitive data is collected from compromised systems and exfiltrated through the established cloud-based C2 infrastructure to support DPRK espionage objectives
